title: Looking Back and Forward
concept: Standard RNNs only see past context; Bidirectional RNNs process text in both directions (left-to-right and right-to-left) to capture future context too.

exercises.prompt: To fully understand the meaning of a word in a sentence, what context do you usually need?

options.text: Only the words that came before it.
Both the words before it and the words after it.
None of the words in the sentence.

exercises.explain: In language, a word's meaning often depends on the words that come after it (the future context), not just the words before it.

cards.title: Can You See the Future?
cards.body: If you only hear the start of a sentence, can you guess the end? Standard AI models read text like a one-way street, looking only at the past. Let's explore how we can give AI the power to look into the future of a sentence to understand it perfectly!

cards.title: What is a Bidirectional RNN?
cards.body: A Bidirectional Recurrent Neural Network (BiRNN) is a model that processes sequence data in two directions: one network reads it from start to finish (left-to-right), and another reads it from finish to start (right-to-left). This allows the AI to understand any word by looking at both its past and future context.

cards.title: The Time-Traveling Reader
cards.body: Imagine reading a mystery novel. If you only read page by page from start to finish, you don't know the future plot twists. But if you could look at the ending while reading the beginning, you get the whole picture! Standard RNNs only read forward (like normal reading), while Bidirectional RNNs read forward and backward at the same time to understand the full story.

cards.title: Solving the Word Riddle
cards.body:
Consider the sentence: "The bank of the river was wet." 

If an AI reads "bank" looking only at the past words ("The..."), it doesn't know if it means a money bank or a river bank. By looking ahead to the future word "river", a Bidirectional RNN immediately knows the correct meaning of "bank".

cards.title: No Real Time-Travel Here!
cards.body: Some think Bidirectional RNNs predict the actual future of the real world. In reality, they only look "forward" into the text you have already provided. They don't predict what will happen tomorrow; they just look at the end of the sentence you already typed!

cards.title: One-Way vs. Two-Way Paths
cards.body: Standard RNNs only travel forward through time, while Bidirectional RNNs travel both forward and backward to get the full picture.

right.text: Reads from left-to-right AND right-to-left. It knows past and future context.

left.text: Reads only from left to right. It only remembers past words.

exercises.prompt: What is the main limitation of a standard, one-way RNN?

options.text: They are too slow to run.
They can only process numbers, not text.
They can only look at past context, not future context.
They ignore the first word of every sentence.

exercises.explain: Standard RNNs process data in one direction, meaning they only have access to past context. They cannot look ahead to see what words are coming next.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A Bidirectional RNN uses both a forward pass and a backward pass to read a sentence.
exercises.explain: Bidirectional RNNs use two separate networks—one for the forward path and one for the backward path—to process both directions.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of this two-way AI model.
exercises.sentence: A [blank] RNN processes text from left-to-right and right-to-left.
exercises.explain: Bidirectional RNNs process data in two directions (forward and backward) to gather context from both sides of a word.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following correctly describes the difference between a Standard RNN and a Bidirectional RNN?

options.text: Standard RNNs can look into the future, while Bidirectional RNNs are stuck in the past.
Bidirectional RNNs look at both past and future words, while Standard RNNs only look at past words.
There is no difference; they are just two names for the same technology.

exercises.explain: Standard RNNs process text in one direction (left-to-right) and only see the past. Bidirectional RNNs process in both directions, capturing both past and future context.

exercises.prompt: How do Bidirectional RNNs solve the limitation of standard RNNs only seeing past context?

options.text: They process the sentence twice as fast by skipping words.
They process the sequence in both directions to use both past and future context.
They translate the text into two different languages.
They convert text into images using two different neural networks.

exercises.explain: Standard RNNs process tokens in one direction, only remembering past tokens. Bidirectional RNNs process text in both directions (left-to-right and right-to-left) to capture both past and future context at every point.
